title: OOD Robustness
objective: Evaluate robustness under distribution shift and distinguish supported effects from null/inconclusive/regression outcomes.

ood_definition.query_shift: OOD queries are sampled from an unseen domain and assigned true_doc_id=-1.
ood_definition.corpus_constraint: Document corpus excludes the OOD domain entirely.

logic: Mark reproducibility as positive when OOD split construction is explicit, parameterized, and tested.
Mark OOD quality gain as null when committed calibration/ablation artifacts show no downstream Kalman-vs-mean gain.
Mark uncertainty-weighting advantage as inconclusive when no dedicated paired OOD significance table is committed yet.
Mark failure mode as regression when OOD query retrieval remains overconfident by construction (no explicit abstention gate in this artifact).
Overall remains inconclusive when positive reproducibility coexists with null gains and unresolved significance evidence.

evidence: OOD dataset generation is explicit and reproducible via create_ood_test_set/create_synthetic_ood_test_set with tested constraints.
positive.notes: Protocol and construction reproducibility are now artifacted.

evidence: Committed uncertainty-calibration and uncertainty-ablation artifacts report zero or limited downstream Kalman-vs-mean quality change.
null.notes: No robust OOD quality uplift is currently demonstrated.

evidence: A dedicated paired OOD significance table for uncertainty-weighted fusion is not yet present in this artifact set.
inconclusive.notes: Directionality is unresolved without direct OOD head-to-head significance outputs.

evidence: OOD queries have true_doc_id=-1, but current pipeline artifacts do not include an abstain/reject decision rule; this is a robustness risk.
regression.notes: Failure-mode regression is tracked as overconfident retrieval risk under strict OOD.

notes: This track is completed as a guarded evidence synthesis with explicit OOD definitions and reproducibility controls.
Claim closure still requires a direct paired OOD benchmark significance artifact.
